title: The Fathers love for His Son. Part 2
scripture_text: ‘’Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John. But John tried to deter him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
devotional:
Jesus replied, “Let it be so now; it is proper for us to do this to fulfil all righteousness.” Then John consented. As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him. And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.”

God as Father in the Bible.

In the Old Testament, there are about 1200 references to father. 15 of these are referring to God, and 13 of the 15 references used of God as father, are talking of God being the father of the nation of Israel not of an individual. The other two references are used in the context of prayer in Jeremiah 3:4,19, again they speaking about a corporate response to God.

In the New Testament, Father was the word Jesus used most often when speaking about or to God, about 170 times. In every prayer except one, Jesus spoke to God as Father. If you read the gospel of John, it could be understood as an exposition of Jesus’ relationship with his Father, as in it there are 109 references to the father.

I just want to mention the word Abba that Jesus used in Mark 14:36 which reads.

Abba, Father,” he said, “everything is possible for you. Take this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will.”

This is the only time we have on record of Jesus using the title Abba. This is in some ways the most difficult time in Jesus’ life, so it is very significant on this occasion. It has been widely taught in Christian circles that the word Abba was used by a young child to communicate with their father, and many see it as a ‘daddy’ word. However, this is an incomplete understanding of the word. The word was also used by adult children as an expression of great honour and respect. Sometimes the Rabbis were called Abba.

There is something in all of us that longs for intimacy, intimacy [with people and with God,] It is a God given need, which is why we need to know God as Abba. The important thing is this, having a right respect for God, and living a life that honours him, which will be the consequence of intimacy with Him.

What kind of relationship did God the father have with Jesus?

This is a very big question, and we could go to lots of places in the bible to try and answer that. So I thought we should take a look at the Baptism of Jesus, and consider what the Father said there, in these words we discover some indication as to how the father related to his Son.

As we consider the Fathers love for Jesus, we need to realise that the love the Father has for his Son Jesus is the same love he has for you and me. God the father does not love Jesus any more that he loves you, nor do he love you any less than he loves Jesus.(John 17 v 23) The relationship the father has with his Son is a prototype, or an example of the kind of relationship he wants to have with us.

We also need to recognise that because many of us have had poor experiences of fatherhood, and we all have had a wrong or incomplete understanding of God, we may find it difficult to accept and embrace the fatherhood of God.

Lets have a look at the text.

Jesus went to John to be baptized. This was very strange because John was baptizing people because they were turning from their sin and turning to God. See Matthew 3:1-12, We know that Jesus had lived a life free from sin, he lived a life in continual fellowship with his father, so why did he want John to baptize him? He chose to be baptized so he could humbly identify with us. His baptism was an expression of profound humility and radical identification.
prayer: Father help me to humbly identify with others.
html:
The Fathers love for His Son. Part 2

Study Text: Matthew 3 13-17
‘’Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John. But John tried to deter him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”

Jesus replied, “Let it be so now; it is proper for us to do this to fulfil all righteousness.” Then John consented. As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him. And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.”

God as Father in the Bible.

In the Old Testament, there are about 1200 references to father. 15 of these are referring to God, and 13 of the 15 references used of God as father, are talking of God being the father of the nation of Israel not of an individual. The other two references are used in the context of prayer in Jeremiah 3:4,19, again they speaking about a corporate response to God.

In the New Testament, Father was the word Jesus used most often when speaking about or to God, about 170 times. In every prayer except one, Jesus spoke to God as Father. If you read the gospel of John, it could be understood as an exposition of Jesus’ relationship with his Father, as in it there are 109 references to the father.

I just want to mention the word Abba that Jesus used in Mark 14:36 which reads.

Abba, Father,” he said, “everything is possible for you. Take this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will.”

This is the only time we have on record of Jesus using the title Abba. This is in some ways the most difficult time in Jesus’ life, so it is very significant on this occasion. It has been widely taught in Christian circles that the word Abba was used by a young child to communicate with their father, and many see it as a ‘daddy’ word. However, this is an incomplete understanding of the word. The word was also used by adult children as an expression of great honour and respect. Sometimes the Rabbis were called Abba.

There is something in all of us that longs for intimacy, intimacy [with people and with God,] It is a God given need, which is why we need to know God as Abba. The important thing is this, having a right respect for God, and living a life that honours him, which will be the consequence of intimacy with Him.

What kind of relationship did God the father have with Jesus?

This is a very big question, and we could go to lots of places in the bible to try and answer that. So I thought we should take a look at the Baptism of Jesus, and consider what the Father said there, in these words we discover some indication as to how the father related to his Son.

As we consider the Fathers love for Jesus, we need to realise that the love the Father has for his Son Jesus is the same love he has for you and me. God the father does not love Jesus any more that he loves you, nor do he love you any less than he loves Jesus.(John 17 v 23) The relationship the father has with his Son is a prototype, or an example of the kind of relationship he wants to have with us.

We also need to recognise that because many of us have had poor experiences of fatherhood, and we all have had a wrong or incomplete understanding of God, we may find it difficult to accept and embrace the fatherhood of God.

Lets have a look at the text.

Jesus went to John to be baptized. This was very strange because John was baptizing people because they were turning from their sin and turning to God. See Matthew 3:1-12, We know that Jesus had lived a life free from sin, he lived a life in continual fellowship with his father, so why did he want John to baptize him? He chose to be baptized so he could humbly identify with us. His baptism was an expression of profound humility and radical identification.

Prayer: Father help me to humbly identify with others.
Bible in a year reading plan: 2 Chronicles 12-15
